item.title: The Parkinson's disease-linked G2019S mutation of LRRK2 increases NADPH oxidase-2 activity in neutrophils for superior control of bacterial infections.
item.description: Cellular & molecular immunology - Allelic variants in the leucine-rich repeat kinase-2 (LRRK2) gene are linked to Parkinson's disease, Crohn's disease and leprosy; however, a consensus role of LRRK2 in infection has not yet emerged...

item.title: International consensus on intestinal epithelial barrier assessment and therapeutic effects in inflammatory bowel diseases clinical trials.
item.description: Gut - Restoration of intestinal barrier integrity and function in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) has been associated not only with a reduced burden of persistent symptoms but also with improved long-te...

item.title: Casemix-based budget impact analysis of ferric derisomaltose versus ferric carboxymaltose in patients with inflammatory bowel disease and iron deficiency anaemia in the Netherlands.
item.description: Journal of medical economics - The prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in the Netherlands is approximately 100,000 individuals currently (578 per 100,000 inhabitant), and is rising. Iron deficiency anaemia (IDA) is co...

item.title: Standardized, repeatable ulcerative colitis histology scoring and endpoint assessment using an automated, foundation model-based tool.
item.description: medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences - In clinical trials for ulcerative colitis (UC), pathologists assess disease severity through standardized histological indices, including the Geboes Score, Robarts Histopathology Index (RHI), and N...
